Satoshi-era miner earns astounding 1231780x return, deposits $3M of Bitcoin to Binance after 14 years of inactivity.
news.summary: A wallet from the Satoshi era, inactive for 14 years, recently came alive to transfer $3 million worth of Bitcoin to the cryptocurrency exchange Binance, potentially for selling. The wallet is linked to a miner who acquired 50 Bitcoins in 2010 when each coin was valued at $0.058, now totaling $2.9. The miner's wallet, dormant for over a decade, moved 50 BTC ($3.05 million) to Binance just 7 hours ago. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: IMF urges US to address fiscal deficit as debt-to-GDP to hit 140% by 2032, exceeding post-WWII levels. Threat to global economy.
news.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has advised the United States to promptly address its increasing fiscal burden. The criticism from the IMF targeted the tax proposals put forth by candidates as the country heads into its first presidential debate. The IMF's annual Article IV health check projections indicate that the U.S. debt-to-GDP ratio is projected to reach 140% by 2032, a notable increase from the current 120.7%. This rise is primarily due to expected fiscal deficits in the coming years and would surpass previous records dating back to the post-World War II era. The IMF has highlighted that these escalating deficits and debt levels present a growing risk to both the U.S. and global economy. The potential consequences include elevated fiscal financing costs and a threat to the smooth rollover of maturing obligations.

news.summary: The latest data reveals that unemployment in the country has reached an all-time high of 14.7%, with over 20 million people out of work. Additionally, the number of confirmed COVID-19 cases has surpassed 1.5 million, with the death toll climbing to over 90,000. Authorities are implementing various measures to combat the economic and health crises, including stimulus packages and social distancing guidelines. news.headline: Enzymes market set to grow to $20.4bn by 2029, driven by food industry demand. Various applications in food, industrial sectors.
news.summary: The global enzymes market is currently valued at USD 14.0 billion in 2024 and is expected to grow to USD 20.4 billion by 2029, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.8%. The growth in the market is primarily attributed to the increasing demand in the food and beverage industry, where enzymes are used to enhance food quality, improve processing efficiency, and extend shelf life. Enzymes like lactase are used in dairy processing to cater to the lactose-intolerant population, while they also play key roles in baking and brewing industries. The market extends to various industrial applications including detergents, textiles, paper and pulp, wastewater treatment, animal feed, cosmetics, and oilfield chemicals. Different types of enzymes are used in these applications, with carbohydrases, proteases, and lipases being some of the primary enzymes employed. The market also sees applications in pharmaceuticals, research, diagnostics, and biocatalysts. The constant innovation in response to consumer demand for high-quality food products is a significant driver for the growth of the enzymes market.

news.headline: Juhan Aguraiuja appointed as Enefit Green Chairman, starts Oct 14. CEO Aavo Kärmas to step down July 1. Board term is 3 years.
news.summary: Juhan Aguraiuja, previously in charge of Adven's Baltic operations, has been named the new Chairman of the Management Board at Enefit Green by the Supervisory Board. Taking over on October 14, he will serve a standard 3-year term as per the Articles of Associations. Aavo Kärmas, the existing CEO of Enefit Green, will step down from his position on July 1, following mutual agreement with the Supervisory Board.
